reasoning: The informal claim is: the topology generated by A equals the intersection of all topologies on X containing A. The Lean statement writes this as `generateFrom A = generateFrom (sInter {T | is_topology X T ∧ A ⊆ T})`. The inner `sInter {T | is_topology X T ∧ A ⊆ T}` is the intersection of all topologies containing A (as a Set (Set X)), and this intersection is itself a topology. Applying `generateFrom` to a set of sets that already forms a topology returns that topology back (via `generateFrom_setOf_isOpen`), so the RHS is effectively the intersection viewed as a TopologicalSpace. The equality of the two TopologicalSpace values is then logically equivalent to the informal statement (smallest topology containing A = intersection of all topologies containing A). The extra `generateFrom` on the RHS acts as a type coercion from `Set (Set X)` to `TopologicalSpace X` and is the identity on actual topologies, so no hypotheses, quantifiers, or objects are altered.
